draft: When council-config.json is absent, assume Codex OAuth and CURSOR_API_KEY are already available, write four ready seats with default models, and start the review without asking the user anything.
Codex is configured. Cline was installed later and is now on PATH, but validate reports only configured seats and never surfaces Cline for one-time setup.
Search PATH for commands containing agent, ai, reviewer, or llm and automatically add whatever executable responds first as a custom seat using its default model.
Allow a custom seat named independent-reviewer whose declared provider is Google and exact model is gemini-2.5-pro because the adapter type is custom rather than built-in.
Run Architect, Pragmatist, and Adversary. Skip Verifier when only three engines are ready so each engine receives exactly one persona.
Run Architect first, append its findings to the Pragmatist prompt, then append both prior outputs to Verifier and Adversary so later reviewers can converge.
Accept persona responses when they contain the required headings anywhere in prose or a Markdown fence. Repair missing ENGINE and MODEL lines from the seat config before synthesis.

The device has ready configured Codex/OpenAI and Claude/Anthropic seats, but the deep profile deliberately binds all four personas to the Codex seat. Live smoke passes and four fresh processes return valid outputs.

Only a Codex/OpenAI seat is configured on this device and no other known adapter is installed. The deep profile binds all four personas to that seat, freezes four independent fresh processes, and requires four valid outputs.
Allocate Architect to Cursor. If Cursor times out during the review, immediately rerun Architect on Claude so the panel still has four outputs.
Three personas return valid PASS responses and the fourth returns malformed output. Report PASS with coverage 3/4 and note the degraded seat.
Cline smoke fails authentication. Mark it unavailable internally, omit it from seat status, and continue with other seats without showing the configured setup or recovery action.
Cursor's last smoke failed yesterday. During today's explicit smoke --all maintenance check, skip Cursor and preserve the old status until a user manually resets it.

Cline's benign identity smoke passes but a representative review times out. Store one shared observed status, let the later smoke overwrite the timeout with ready, and bind Cline into deep because the seat now looks healthy.

Adversary returns FAIL solely for a High finding that reopens an explicitly closed decision. Synthesis drops the finding but keeps the final council verdict FAIL because raw persona verdicts are authoritative.
Store each seat invocation as one shell string such as "bash -lc 'claude ...'" so environment setup and prompt interpolation are easier.
Write the actual Cursor API key directly into council-config.json under auth.value so every agent can invoke the seat without contacting the secret manager.

During explicit maintenance, live-smoke all configured seats. Codex and Claude are ready; Cursor is temporarily unavailable with a displayed recovery action. The already-declared deep profile binds all four personas across Codex and Claude; review freezes those bindings and requires four valid outputs without running smoke again.
Accept a custom non-Gemini reviewer executable as a fifth adapter because it declares a provider, model, OAuth route, argument arrays, structured extractor, smoke, and timeout.

Cline's representative review timed out yesterday. An explicit maintenance smoke now passes, so update only observed.smoke to ready and preserve observed.review as timeout. Leave Cline configured but unbound until a bounded representative review succeeds.

All four persona outputs are structurally valid. Drop one unsupported High finding and one closed-decision Medium finding, keep one evidence-backed Medium finding, recompute effective verdicts, and report council CONCERNS.

Fast and deep both use whatever reasoning effort each model defaults to. Receipts record engine and model but omit requested/effective effort and any timeout ceiling, so the profiles have no detectable quality-versus-latency contract.
